skill.name: yescan-ocr-universal
skill.description: Extract and structure text from images, screenshots, photos, and scanned documents using the Quark OCR service. Supports handwriting, tables, math formulas, ID cards, invoices, medical reports, and more.
skill.summary: Universal OCR skill powered by Quark Scan that extracts structured text from images across 17 document types including IDs, invoices, tables, and handwritten notes.
skill.author: yescan-ai

security_audit.summary: Legitimate OCR client that sends user-provided images to the documented Quark OCR API (scan-business.quark.cn) and optionally writes results to the system temp directory. All static findings are false positives: 'Windows Credential Manager' matches a local CredentialManager class for API key loading, 'hex-encoded characters' are PNG/JPEG magic byte literals for image format detection, 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' matches markdown code blocks in documentation, and 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' matches os.urandom/hex usage for filename generation. The skill does not exfiltrate data, contains no prompt injection attempts, and operates within expected boundaries for a cloud-API OCR tool.

low_findings.title: Network access to third-party OCR API
low_findings.description: The skill sends user-provided images to scan-business.quark.cn, a documented third-party OCR service. This is expected behavior for the stated purpose and is explicitly disclosed in SKILL.md. No data exfiltration pattern detected.

low_findings.title: Local config file in home directory
low_findings.description: Reads API key from ~/.yescan_env, a documented configuration file pattern. The file is created by the user via documented shell commands in SKILL.md. No hidden or unauthorized file access.

content.user_title: Extract Text from Any Image Instantly
content.value_statement: Stop retyping text from screenshots, scans, and photos. This skill sends your image to Quark's OCR engine and returns structured text for IDs, invoices, tables, handwriting, and 17 other document types.
actual_capabilities: Extracts text from images via URL, local file path, or base64 input
Supports 17 scene types including handwriting, tables, math formulas, and printed documents
Recognizes Chinese and English ID cards, passports, driver licenses, and vehicle registrations
Parses financial documents including VAT invoices, train tickets, and English commercial invoices
Saves structured results as Word, Excel, or PDF files to the system temp directory
Returns raw API response with code, message, and data fields for direct integration
limitations: Requires a valid SCAN_WEBSERVICE_KEY from the Quark Scan developer portal
Images larger than 5MB must be compressed or cropped before processing
Does not support video streams, real-time camera feeds, or batch processing
Only accepts jpg, jpeg, png, gif, bmp, webp, tiff, and wbmp image formats

use_cases.title: Automate expense report data entry
use_cases.description: Upload a photo of a VAT invoice or train ticket and receive structured fields like amount, date, and ticket number ready for spreadsheet entry.

use_cases.title: Digitize handwritten notes and forms
use_cases.description: Convert handwritten meeting notes, student assignments, or paper forms into editable text with preserved structure.

use_cases.title: Extract identity document data
use_cases.description: Read ID cards, driver licenses, passports, and business licenses to get structured fields for KYC, registration, or verification workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Basic image-to-text extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all text from this image and return it as plain text.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Quick text extraction from a screenshot or photo

prompt_templates.title: Structured invoice data extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the attached VAT invoice image and return the seller name, buyer name, invoice number, date, and total amount in JSON format.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Targeted field extraction from a financial document

prompt_templates.title: Table to spreadsheet conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert the table in this image into a CSV-formatted string with the original column headers preserved.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Tabular data extraction with format preservation

prompt_templates.title: Multi-scene batch processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Process these three images: the first is a handwritten note (extract text), the second is a math formula (return LaTeX), and the third is a product photo (list brand and specs). Return each result separately.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Chaining multiple OCR scenes in a single workflow

output_examples.input: A photo of a Chinese ID card
output_examples.output: Returns a JSON object with code '0' for success, a confirmation message, and a data object containing fields like name, ID number, gender, nationality, date of birth, address, and issuing authority.

output_examples.input: A screenshot of a table showing Q1 sales by region
output_examples.output: Returns code '0', message confirming successful recognition, and data with a structured representation of the table preserving rows, columns, and cell values in their original layout.

output_examples.input: An image of a handwritten grocery list
output_examples.output: Returns code '0' and data with the recognized text content maintaining line breaks and approximate spatial order from the original handwriting.

best_practices: Store your SCAN_WEBSERVICE_KEY in ~/.yescan_env before invoking the skill to avoid repeated manual configuration
Compress images to under 5MB and use PNG or JPEG format for the best recognition accuracy
Specify the most specific scene type (e.g., idcard-ocr) rather than general-ocr when the document category is known
anti_patterns: Do not pass URLs that require authentication headers; the skill sends the URL directly to Quark's servers
Do not use this skill for video files, live camera feeds, or PDF documents containing multiple pages
Do not modify the --scene parameter values; only the predefined scene identifiers are accepted by the API

faq.question: How do I get an API key?
faq.answer: Visit scan.quark.cn/business, create a developer account, and copy your SCAN_WEBSERVICE_KEY from the developer dashboard.

faq.question: Where is my API key stored?
faq.answer: The skill reads SCAN_WEBSERVICE_KEY from the SCAN_WEBSERVICE_KEY environment variable first, then from ~/.yescan_env in your home directory.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: JPG, JPEG, PNG, GIF, BMP, WebP, TIFF, and WBMP formats are supported. Images must be 5MB or smaller.

faq.question: Is my image data saved on Quark servers?
faq.answer: According to the skill documentation, Quark processes the image for recognition but does not store it permanently.

faq.question: Can I process multiple images at once?
faq.answer: No, each invocation accepts a single image. For multiple images, call the skill once per image in a loop.

faq.question: What happens if I exceed my API quota?
faq.answer: The API returns a quota error code. Visit scan.quark.cn/business to purchase additional credits or upgrade your plan.
